steps.title: Identify the structures on each side
steps.content: The left side is an independent clause ending with to material (which needs an object). The right side is an independent clause with subject her thought provoking works and verb were.

steps.title: Apply the punctuation rule
steps.content: A semicolon separates two independent clauses. Choice A correctly provides a semicolon and the noun wastes to complete the prepositional phrase. Choices C and D violate this rule (comma splice and run-on sentence, respectively), while choice B creates syntactically awkward parallelism. That matches choice A — A is correct.
